name: Cloud Run MCP
what_it_does: MCP server that enables AI agents to deploy applications to Google Cloud Run. Provides tools to deploy file contents or local folders, list and inspect Cloud Run services, retrieve service logs, list GCP projects, and create new projects. Supports local and remote (on Cloud Run itself) deployment modes.
use_cases: Deploying applications to Cloud Run directly from an AI-powered IDE or CLI
Listing and inspecting running Cloud Run services and their logs
AI-driven infrastructure management for GCP serverless workloads
Managing multiple GCP projects and services via natural language
not_for: Complex multi-service orchestration beyond Cloud Run
Production CI/CD pipelines (better suited as a developer convenience tool)
best_when: You want AI agents to deploy and manage Google Cloud Run services with minimal friction, especially during prototyping or iterative development.
avoid_when: You need multi-cloud deployment, complex orchestration, or are not using Google Cloud Platform.

auth.notes: Requires Google Cloud SDK authentication via 'gcloud auth login' and 'gcloud auth application-default login'. Remote deployments use IAM-authenticated proxy. OAuth supported via gcloud SDK OAuth client.

pricing.free_tier_limits: MCP server is free; Cloud Run usage billed by GCP (has its own free tier)
pricing.notes: Apache 2.0 licensed. Cloud Run itself has a generous free tier but requires a GCP billing account for production use.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Logs tool provides error messages from Cloud Run services. DNS rebinding protection available but disabled by default.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Deploys overwrite existing services by name, making redeployments idempotent. But create-project is not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Some tools (deploy-local-folder, list-projects, create-project) only available in local mode, not remote
Remote MCP server restricted to same GCP project as the server itself
DNS rebinding protection (ENABLE_HOST_VALIDATION) is disabled by default
SKIP_IAM_CHECK defaults to true, which may lead to permission errors at deploy time rather than preflight
Requires GCP project and billing account setup before first use

security_score_components.security_notes: Google Cloud IAM with Cloud Run-specific permissions. Workload Identity for service authentication. SOC2, ISO27001, FedRAMP. Serverless containers — automatic TLS, no infrastructure to manage. VPC connector for private network access.
